skill.name: visual-reviewer
skill.description: Automatically takes screenshots of visual outputs (web pages, documents, UIs) and analyzes them for layout, spacing, typography, color, and interaction issues, then fixes discovered problems in the source code.
skill.summary: Automated visual quality review with screenshot capture, issue detection, and auto-fix for web pages, documents, and UI interfaces.
skill.author: Barry-Liu-888

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 64 patterns across 5 files (849 lines). After semantic review, all 64 findings are confirmed false positives: markdown code block formatting in documentation triggered shell-execution patterns, Win32 API calls for screenshot functionality triggered system-reconnaissance patterns, and bilingual (Chinese/English) content triggered entropy heuristics. The skill performs exactly its stated purpose — screenshot capture and visual quality analysis — with no malicious intent, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. Screen capture and window enumeration are inherent to the functionality and carry minor privacy implications, warranting a low risk classification.

low_findings.title: Screen Capture Capability with Privacy Implications
low_findings.description: The skill captures screenshots of user screens including browser windows, document viewers, and arbitrary screen regions. While this is the skill's stated purpose, screenshots may inadvertently capture sensitive information visible on screen. The captured images are saved locally and not exfiltrated.

low_findings.title: Window Enumeration Reveals Running Applications
low_findings.description: The screenshot scripts enumerate visible windows and running processes to locate target windows for capture. This reveals what applications are running, which is inherent to the window-targeting functionality but has minor privacy implications.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick Code Blocks Flagged as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 50+ locations in SKILL.md and visual_checklist.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All are markdown code block delimiters (backticks) used for formatting code examples in documentation. No actual command execution via backticks exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: Win32 API Screen Capture Flagged as Multiple Threats
dangerous_patterns.description: Python and PowerShell scripts use Win32 API calls (GetWindowRect, EnumWindows, ClientToScreen, SetForegroundWindow) for window identification and screenshot capture. These were flagged as system reconnaissance, network reconnaissance, screen capture upload, and weak cryptography — all false positives. These are legitimate API calls necessary for precise window-level screenshot functionality.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bilingual Content Flagged as High File Entropy
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md and visual_checklist.md contain Chinese and English technical documentation with code examples. The mixed-language content triggered entropy heuristics (6.26 and 6.78 bits). This is normal for bilingual documentation and not indicative of obfuscation or encrypted content.

dangerous_patterns.title: PowerShell String Interpolation Flagged as Command Substitution
dangerous_patterns.description: PowerShell variable interpolation syntax ($var, ${var}) in debug output strings was flagged as shell command substitution. These are string formatting operations in Write-Output and debug messages, not command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Localhost URL Flagged as Hardcoded Network Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md line 43 references http://localhost:8765 for starting a local development server to preview HTML files. This is standard practice for local web development and does not connect to external servers.

content.user_title: Review and Auto-Fix Visual Quality Issues in Your UI
content.value_statement: After producing visual outputs like web pages or documents, this skill automatically takes screenshots and analyzes them for layout, spacing, typography, color, and interaction issues. It then fixes the discovered problems in your source code without waiting for manual review.
actual_capabilities: Take screenshots of specific windows, browser content, or screen regions using Win32 API
Analyze visual quality across seven dimensions: layout, spacing, typography, color, interaction, data display, and edge cases
Score each visual dimension on a 0 to 3 scale with detailed acceptance criteria
Auto-fix CSS and layout issues directly in source code based on screenshot analysis
Generate visual review reports with before and after comparisons for each fix
Support multiple capture modes: fullscreen, active window, window by title, window by process name, and screen region
limitations: Screenshot capture uses Windows Win32 API and works best on Windows; macOS and Linux support is limited
Cannot test interactive states like hover effects or CSS animations through static screenshots alone
Visual analysis accuracy depends on the AI vision model's ability to interpret screenshot details
Does not replace formal accessibility audits or WCAG compliance testing

use_cases.title: Rapid UI Quality Assurance
use_cases.description: Frontend developers can automatically review HTML and CSS output for alignment, spacing, and responsive design issues before pushing code. Screenshots are captured at multiple viewport widths and checked against a comprehensive visual checklist.

use_cases.title: Document Formatting Review
use_cases.description: Technical writers can check markdown documents, tables, and code blocks for visual consistency and formatting problems. The skill captures rendered document views and checks spacing, typography, and data display.

use_cases.title: Design Implementation Verification
use_cases.description: UI designers can verify that implemented interfaces match design specifications by comparing screenshots against a detailed visual checklist covering layout, color coordination, and interaction states.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Visual Review of an HTML Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the visual quality of this HTML page. Take a screenshot and check for layout, spacing, and alignment issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: You just created an HTML page and want a quick visual quality check

prompt_templates.title: Typography and Color Contrast Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Take a screenshot of the browser window showing my page and check for typography hierarchy issues and color contrast problems against WCAG AA standards.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to ensure text is readable and font sizes create a clear visual hierarchy

prompt_templates.title: Full Visual Audit with Auto-Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full visual review on this page. Check all seven dimensions: layout, spacing, typography, color, interaction, data display, and edge cases. Auto-fix any issues found and regenerate the screenshot to verify.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want a comprehensive review where the skill both finds and fixes issues

prompt_templates.title: Responsive Layout Review Across Breakpoints
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the responsive layout at 320 pixels, 768 pixels, and 1440 pixels viewport widths. Fix any issues found at each breakpoint, then generate a comparison report showing the page at all three sizes.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to verify your page works correctly on mobile, tablet, and desktop screens

output_examples.input: Review the visual quality of this login form page
output_examples.output: Found 3 issues: (1) Submit button is misaligned with the input fields, fixed by adding vertical-align: middle to the button. (2) Error message text has low contrast at #999 on white background, changed to #666. (3) Password field padding is 8px while email field uses 12px, unified both to 12px for consistency.

output_examples.input: Check the dashboard page for responsive issues at mobile size
output_examples.output: At 320px width, the sidebar overlaps the main content area. Applied a media query to collapse the sidebar into a hamburger menu below 768px breakpoint. The data table overflows horizontally, added overflow-x: auto to the table container with a min-width fallback.

output_examples.input: Review the markdown report document for formatting issues
output_examples.output: Found 2 issues: (1) Code blocks in section 3 lack language tags, making syntax highlighting inconsistent. Added language identifiers. (2) The comparison table in section 5 has uneven column widths, the first column is too narrow causing text wrapping. Adjusted column proportions to 40/30/30.

best_practices: Always take a baseline screenshot before making any visual fixes so you can compare before and after states
Run the visual review after every significant UI change, not just at the end of a development session
Review screenshots at multiple viewport widths including 320 pixels, 768 pixels, and 1440 pixels to catch responsive layout issues
anti_patterns: Do not skip the screenshot step and try to evaluate visual quality by reading source code alone, visual issues are often invisible in code
Do not apply visual fixes without re-screening afterward to confirm the changes actually resolved the problem
Do not change business logic, JavaScript functionality, or backend code when fixing visual issues; only modify CSS and layout properties

faq.question: What types of visual outputs can this skill review?
faq.answer: It can review HTML pages, web applications, markdown documents, presentations, and any visual output that can be opened in a browser or document viewer and captured via screenshot.

faq.question: Does this skill work on macOS or Linux operating systems?
faq.answer: The Python screenshot script uses Windows Win32 API for precise window-level capture. Full functionality works best on Windows. Some capture modes may work on other platforms but window targeting by title or process name is Windows-specific.

faq.question: How does the skill decide which visual issues to fix automatically?
faq.answer: Issues scored 0 or 1 on the visual checklist are considered serious and are fixed automatically. Issues scored 2 are flagged for review but left for you to decide. Issues scored 3 need no action.

faq.question: Can this skill test interactive states like hover effects or animations?
faq.answer: Screenshots capture static visual states only. Testing hover, focus, active, and animation states requires manual browser interaction or additional tools. The skill can review the CSS rules for these states but cannot trigger them.

faq.question: What visual dimensions does the review checklist cover?
faq.answer: The checklist covers seven dimensions: layout alignment, spacing consistency, typography hierarchy, color contrast and coordination, interaction states, data display formatting, and edge case handling including empty states and extreme screen sizes.

faq.question: Does the skill modify my original source code files?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill directly edits CSS and layout code to fix visual issues it discovers. It only changes style properties and layout rules. Business logic, JavaScript functionality, and backend code are never modified.
